title: Harness Engineering: What Separates Top Agentic Engineers Right Now
summary: Harness engineering is the practice of building the orchestration layer around coding agents that comprises approximately 98% of their effectiveness. It operates at two levels: rules and hooks within a single AI session, and multi-session orchestration that breaks complex tasks into focused workflows to enable reliable scaling of agentic systems.
writeup: This talk defines harness engineering as the practice of building the AI layer wrapped around a coding agent: the global rules, skills, MCP servers, code-base search tools, hooks, and sub-agents that give an LLM its actual capabilities beyond raw text generation. The presenter argues it is a direct evolution of context engineering, with one genuinely new element: control flow, meaning loops and orchestration across multiple coding agent sessions. The central through-line is a mindset shift from blaming the model for mistakes to treating every failure as a signal to improve the harness, so the system compounds in quality over time rather than waiting for the next model version.

similar.title: Harness Engineering Is AI’s New Gold Rush

key_points: A large language model alone cannot access a filesystem, run Git commands, or execute shell commands; those capabilities come from the first harness layer built into tools like Claude Code and Codex, not from the model itself.
The AI layer, which the developer actually controls, consists of six components: global rules, skills, MCP servers, code-base search (LSP or knowledge graphs), hooks, and sub-agents.

Harness engineering is mostly continuous with context engineering for five of its six components; the true evolution is in the sixth, control, specifically loops and orchestration of multiple coding agent sessions chained into larger workflows.

The harness engineering mindset reframes every agent mistake as an opportunity to improve the system: if the agent lacked a convention, it goes in AGENTS.md; if it ran a destructive command, a hook blocks it next time, so quality compounds session over session.

System evolution works through a feedback loop: initial context and principles feed the agent, hooks and review agents act as sensors, skills provide self-correction, and the AI layer is updated forward so each future session inherits the lessons of past failures.

chapters.title: The Diagram: LLM, Tool Layer, AI Layer
